title: The Girl Who Slept Too Little

jokes.text: What are they cock-a-doodily doing?
jokes.setup: Early morning construction noise complaint
jokes.punchline: Flanders combines 'cock-a-doodle-doo' with his signature 'diddly' speech pattern
jokes.explanation: Perfect Flanders character integration - only he would make this specific wordplay blend

jokes.text: Flanders, I can't listen to your crap before my coffee. / Okily-dokily, here's some french roast.
jokes.setup: Homer rudely dismisses Flanders
jokes.punchline: Flanders cheerfully produces coffee immediately, being impossibly accommodating
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer-Flanders dynamic with nice physical comedy element

jokes.text: Now, I'll go make toast. / Stupid best friend flanders.
jokes.setup: Flanders continues being helpful
jokes.punchline: Homer mutters complaint while acknowledging Flanders is his best friend
jokes.explanation: Homer's internal conflict about Flanders perfectly captured

jokes.text: Stamps? Those are for snail mail! / Stamps rule! I mean suck!
jokes.setup: Lisa mentions stamps dismissively
jokes.punchline: Bart tries to be cool but accidentally reveals he actually likes stamps
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Bart's insecurity about appearing uncool

jokes.text: What ever happened to 'please' and 'thank you'? / I think they killed each other. You know, one of those murder-suicide deals.
jokes.setup: Marge asks rhetorical question about lost politeness
jokes.punchline: Homer gives darkly literal explanation treating manners as people
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist logic with perfect dark comedy timing - very quotable

jokes.text: The noise is jiggling my grapes to the bottom of the jell-O.
jokes.setup: Complaint about construction noise
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific domestic imagery of jiggling Jell-O mold
jokes.explanation: Very Marge-specific complaint with nice visual comedy

jokes.text: What man would want you now?
jokes.setup: Marge's Jell-O complaint
jokes.punchline: Homer's inappropriately harsh response
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer insensitivity but not particularly creative

jokes.text: Seven, four, three, one... oh, wait, that's my pin number.
jokes.setup: Homer attempts to lead a chant
jokes.punchline: Accidentally reveals personal banking information
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer stupidity with good misdirection structure

jokes.text: 'come home from Iraq,' my wife says. 'Fallujah's too dangerous,' my wife says.
jokes.setup: Soldier complaining about wife's concerns about war
jokes.punchline: Ironic that he's now dealing with cemetery construction noise instead
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about war vs domestic annoyances, but feels somewhat disconnected

jokes.text: You know, why didn't you just say 'stamp collectors'? / 'Cause I'm tired of dumbing myself down for you.
jokes.setup: Someone uses fancy word 'philatelists'
jokes.punchline: Admits to deliberately dumbing down for Homer
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about Homer's intelligence level

jokes.text: The postal service is sending a change of address card... to itself.
jokes.setup: News report about postal service moving
jokes.punchline: Bureaucratic absurdity of mailing itself address change
jokes.explanation: Clever bureaucratic absurdity - very quotable one-liner

jokes.text: When I die, you can mourn me from the bathroom. That's multitasking.
jokes.setup: Living next to cemetery discussion
jokes.punchline: Homer finds efficiency benefit in proximity to his grave
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic mixing morbid practicality with modern buzzwords

jokes.text: I don't even like watching the count on sesame street. One coconut, two coconuts, three coconets...
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining her fear of spooky things
jokes.punchline: Transforms Count Dracula's counting into innocent coconuts
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing Lisa's attempt to make scary things less scary

jokes.text: Go back to your own country!
jokes.setup: Lisa doing innocent Count Dracula impression
jokes.punchline: Homer's xenophobic response to Transylvanian accent
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer prejudice but the timing and context make it absurd rather than offensive

jokes.text: Then the zombies will eat you first, and you won't have to watch them eat us. / Thanks for making me feel better. / Well, thanks for making me feel better, knowing that your screams when the zombies chomp your brains will warn me so I can get away.
jokes.setup: Lisa worried about zombies
jokes.punchline: Bart's increasingly elaborate explanation of how her death benefits him
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating dark comedy with perfect sibling dynamic

jokes.text: There's no such thing as zombies! / Glad to hear you say that, because the person who doesn't believe in zombies is always the first to get feasted upon.
jokes.setup: Lisa declares zombies aren't real
jokes.punchline: Bart cites horror movie trope about non-believers dying first
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-reference to horror genre conventions

jokes.text: So that's why I've been smelling margaritas at 3:00 A.M. / Hey, I love waking up drunk in the morning.
jokes.setup: Marge discovers Homer's bedside drinks
jokes.punchline: Homer cheerfully admits to alcoholic morning routine
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer alcoholism humor - in character but not particularly creative

jokes.text: Wait a minute, there's no batteries in this thing. / I needed them for my remote-control helicopter.
jokes.setup: Baby monitor not working
jokes.punchline: Bart casually admits to stealing batteries for toy
jokes.explanation: Good reveal of why they can't hear Maggie crying

jokes.text: There's a little plastic man in your hair, and I'm trying to rescue him. / No soldier left behind.
jokes.setup: Homer has toy helicopter crash in his hair
jokes.punchline: Treats rescue mission with military seriousness
jokes.explanation: Nice physical comedy with Homer's tendency to over-dramatize

jokes.text: Lisa, when you've sustained as many blows to the head as I have, consistency is something, something that something... / I love you, Bart.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to explain his inconsistency
jokes.punchline: Loses train of thought mid-sentence and says something random to wrong kid
jokes.explanation: Perfect demonstration of Homer's brain damage affecting his speech patterns

jokes.text: Hey, Simpsons. That'll be 40 bucks. / Oh, I thought it was 20! / Yeah, that's per axle. Park over there in Carl four. No early outs.
jokes.setup: Lenny charging for parking at stamp museum
jokes.punchline: Reveals elaborate parking scheme with per-axle pricing and designated spots
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing working-class entrepreneurship with escalating details

jokes.text: Living next to the stamp museum is making me a mint! I bought a diamond for my belly button!
jokes.setup: Lenny making money from parking
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'mint' plus ridiculous luxury purchase
jokes.explanation: Decent pun combined with absurd spending choice that fits blue-collar character suddenly getting money

jokes.text: Living next to the stamp museum is making me a mint! I bought a diamond for my belly button! You guys need water? / No one's gonna pay a buck to drink water from your hose, so... / Pay the man, Homer.
jokes.setup: Lenny bragging about his profits
jokes.punchline: Homer dismisses overpriced hose water, Marge immediately contradicts him
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Lenny's entrepreneurship and family dynamic

jokes.text: Living next to the stamp museum is making me a mint! I bought a diamond for my belly button!
jokes.setup: Lenny explaining his parking business success
jokes.punchline: 'Making me a mint' pun while describing ridiculous belly button jewelry
jokes.explanation: Decent pun combined with absurd spending choice

jokes.text: Yeesh! Butter face.
jokes.setup: Katherine Blodgett stamp tribute
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude comment about historical figure's appearance
jokes.explanation: Lazy humor relying on crude appearance joke - very much in character but low-effort writing

jokes.text: You stole it from me, Elisha Gray. Read the patent number, bitch!
jokes.setup: Educational stamp display about telephone invention
jokes.punchline: Historical figure uses modern profanity in patent dispute
jokes.explanation: Excellent anachronistic humor with historical figures acting contemporary

jokes.text: You read a book all the way through? Why?
jokes.setup: Someone mentions reading a complete book
jokes.punchline: Homer genuinely confused by concept of finishing books
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer anti-intellectualism with genuine bewilderment

jokes.text: Is this the land of the wild beasts? / No, this is the land of the wild feasts! The Hillside Wrangler steak house!
jokes.setup: Reference to children's book 'Where the Wild Things Are'
jokes.punchline: Transforms into restaurant commercial with 'beasts' to 'feasts' pun
jokes.explanation: Clever parody of classic children's literature for commercial purposes

jokes.text: And I'm jumbo shrimp! Maximum two servings! / That's bull... Man!
jokes.setup: Cute talking food characters in commercial
jokes.punchline: Jumbo shrimp almost swears about portion limits
jokes.explanation: Decent fake-out profanity in children's commercial context

jokes.text: I wanted to be a children's book illustrator ever since Playboy wouldn't publish my cartoons because they were too filthy.
jokes.setup: Children's book author explaining his career path
jokes.punchline: His work was rejected by Playboy for being too explicit
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor - if Playboy rejected content as too filthy, what's he putting in kids' books?

jokes.text: I've been digging graves for 30 years, and I've never buried anyone alive. If I did, they'd ring this bell. / Well, that's just the wind. / Oh, that's just a tree branch, with a nice watch on it!
jokes.setup: Gravedigger claims never to have buried anyone alive
jokes.punchline: Increasingly suspicious explanations for bell ringing and tree branch 'jewelry'
jokes.explanation: Dark escalating comedy with increasingly implausible explanations

jokes.text: What a lousy party! I don't care what apu was in a past life, in this life, he's a blabbermouth! And that Dr. Hibbert was so boring. 'Homer, we've got to get that lump checked out.' 'Homer, we must discuss your test results.' 'Homer, we've got to find you a donor.'
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about boring party guests
jokes.punchline: Escalating medical concerns that Homer finds tedious rather than alarming
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character work - treating serious medical issues as social annoyances

jokes.text: You see, sweetie, when a mommy and daddy are married for years and have nothing new to talk about, they bad-mouth their closest friends.
jokes.setup: Lisa confused about adult behavior
jokes.punchline: Marge's brutally honest explanation of marriage dynamics
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observational humor about marriage - painfully accurate and well-delivered

jokes.text: What has two ears but can't hear? / Grandpa! / Tragically, yes.
jokes.setup: Homer's riddle game
jokes.punchline: Dark family truth delivered as riddle answer
jokes.explanation: Good dark family humor turning elderly relative's disability into riddle game with matter-of-fact acceptance

jokes.text: But don't be surprised if a snuggle monster shows up. / Well, I hope he's accompanied by the 'how was your day' monster and the 'foot rub' monster, and the 'let me just....' / don't worry, he's not showing up.
jokes.setup: Homer hints at romantic intentions
jokes.punchline: Marge lists other attention she wants, Homer immediately backs down
jokes.explanation: Great marital dynamic humor with realistic relationship negotiations

jokes.text: What about Bart's bedroom? / You know that race car bed I made him? The brakes are shot.
jokes.setup: Looking for safe bedroom alternatives
jokes.punchline: Homer applies literal car mechanics to furniture
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor - treating decorative car bed as actual vehicle

jokes.text: Mom, I think there's a danger this time they might lose the ponderosa.
jokes.setup: Lisa watching old Bonanza rerun
jokes.punchline: Genuinely worried about outcome of show that aired decades ago
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing Lisa's earnest engagement with old TV

jokes.text: Why you very little...!
jokes.setup: Home movies of family
jokes.punchline: Classic Homer strangling Bart caught on camera
jokes.explanation: Classic callback to running gag - not original but satisfying for fans

jokes.text: Well worth the $4,000. / $4,000? / Now, Marge, when our little girl's happiness is at stake... / Run, Marge! Start the car!
jokes.setup: Homer seemingly willing to pay for therapy
jokes.punchline: Immediately panics and flees when he hears the price
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer misdirection - talks big until money is involved

jokes.text: I'm not chasing you, Mr. Simpson. / And you won't! Unless you can leap through fire!
jokes.setup: Therapist calmly states he's not pursuing them
jokes.punchline: Homer creates elaborate escape scenario with fire obstacle
jokes.explanation: Good Homer paranoia and over-the-top escape planning

jokes.text: I don't work here. I was just visiting another security guard that died. Bye, now.
jokes.setup: Guard at cemetery gate
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of visiting dead colleague
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor delivered with matter-of-fact tone about workplace mortality

jokes.text: Hi, Dr. Nick. / Hi, everybody! / I mean, I'm not dr. Nick. I'M... Dr. Octopus. I'm going to get you, spidey. Then I will have upside-down kiss with Mary Jane.
jokes.setup: Dr. Nick trying to disguise himself
jokes.punchline: Terrible Spider-Man villain impression with broken English
jokes.explanation: Classic Dr. Nick incompetence with pop culture references

jokes.text: Bye, Dr. Nick. / Bye, Lisa. And remember, you have a checkup next thursday. / We don't go to you anymore. We have a better doctor. / Oh, congratulations!
jokes.setup: Dr. Nick maintaining appointment despite disguise
jokes.punchline: His sincere congratulations on getting better medical care
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dr. Nick character moment - his genuine happiness about patients getting better care shows unexpected professional ethics

jokes.text: You know, I actually got into law school, but I just couldn't afford it. / Quiet, I hear something. / Yeah, that's my life slipping away.
jokes.setup: Chief Wiggum sharing personal backstory during investigation
jokes.punchline: Realizes the sound he hears is his existential crisis
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly deep and dark humor from Wiggum's character development

jokes.text: Don't worry, we're not evil spirits! / Although, we were evil spirits, we'd probably assume the shape of your parents.
jokes.setup: Parents trying to reassure Lisa they're not ghosts
jokes.punchline: Immediately undermine their own reassurance with horror movie logic
jokes.explanation: Great self-defeating logic that makes their reassurance even more frightening

jokes.text: I don't know how I reproduce, but we'll have a great time figuring it out.
jokes.setup: Monster proposing to Lisa
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo from blob creature
jokes.explanation: Adult humor hidden in children's nightmare sequence - monster's biological confusion creates innuendo

jokes.text: For god's sake, girl, you're eight years old. It's natural for you to feel scared sometime. / But I'm too smart to get scared.
jokes.setup: Monster giving Lisa reasonable advice
jokes.punchline: Lisa's intellectual pride prevents her from accepting normal childhood fear
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character work - her intelligence becomes a barrier to normal emotional development

jokes.text: You could just draw the blinds, you silly goose. / Or get a night-light. / My dad says they're too expensive. / For god's sake, they're four bucks apiece.
jokes.setup: Monsters offering practical solutions to Lisa's fears
jokes.punchline: Reveal Homer's extreme cheapness over minimal expense
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven humor about Homer's ridiculous penny-pinching

jokes.text: You could just draw the blinds, you silly goose. / Or get a night-light. / My dad says they're too expensive. / For god's sake, they're four bucks apiece.
jokes.setup: Lisa's fear of cemetery view
jokes.punchline: Monsters' practical solutions vs. Homer's cheapness
jokes.explanation: Great subversion - nightmare monsters providing more practical parenting than Homer's extreme cheapness

jokes.text: What is it about slime that chicks don't dig?
jokes.setup: Monster trying to understand romantic rejection
jokes.punchline: Universal dating confusion applied to monster physiology
jokes.explanation: Fun application of dating humor to monster characteristics

jokes.text: We're always just a concussion away!
jokes.setup: Monsters saying goodbye to Lisa
jokes.punchline: Cheerful reminder that head trauma brings them back
jokes.explanation: Dark but cheerful callback to Homer's brain damage theme

jokes.text: Uh, chief, it's light out. You can open your eyes and come down. / I don't want to, Lou! / I've got a pizza bagel for you. / Let's roll.
jokes.setup: Chief Wiggum still hiding from cemetery investigation
jokes.punchline: Immediately motivated by junk food offer
jokes.explanation: Good character payoff showing Wiggum's simple motivations and cowardice
